conflict: Police stop Micah and Dre after a nearby theft because they match the description before either boy has spoken.
consequence: Micah is released but bloodied and publicly seen against the cruiser, hardening his distrust and costing Marcia clinic money she does not have.
must_land_beats: The stop shows procedure as theater and violence as routine.
Dre's fear registers under the jokes.
Neighbor witnesses matter because shame becomes communal.
Micah's body carries the chapter's proof.
objective: Pick up school paperwork and get home without worsening anything.
